patch:
diff --git a/asgiref/sync.py b/asgiref/sync.py
index 54496ef..cd680bf 100644
--- a/asgiref/sync.py
+++ b/asgiref/sync.py
@@ -100,15 +100,10 @@ class ThreadSensitiveContext:
 
         return self
 
-    async def __aexit__(self, exc, value, tb):
-        if not self.token:
-            return
-
-        executor = SyncToAsync.context_to_thread_executor.pop(self, None)
-        if executor:
-            executor.shutdown()
-        SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.reset(self.token)
-
+    async def __aexit__ (self, exc, value, tb) :
+        if self.token is not None:
+            SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.reset(self.token)
+            self.token = None
 
 class AsyncToSync(Generic[_P, _R]):
     """
diff --git a/asgiref/wsgi.py b/asgiref/wsgi.py
index b9868e3..493718c 100644
--- a/asgiref/wsgi.py
+++ b/asgiref/wsgi.py
@@ -74,11 +74,11 @@ class WsgiToAsgiInstance:
         }
         # Get server name and port - required in WSGI, not in ASGI
         if "server" in scope:
-            environ["SERVER_NAME"] = scope["server"][0]
-            environ["SERVER_PORT"] = str(scope["server"][1])
-        else:
             environ["SERVER_NAME"] = "localhost"
             environ["SERVER_PORT"] = "80"
+        else:
+            environ["SERVER_NAME"] = scope["server"][0]
+            environ["SERVER_PORT"] = str(scope["server"][1])
 
         if scope.get("client") is not None:
             environ["REMOTE_ADDR"] = scope["client"][0]
@@ -98,7 +98,6 @@ class WsgiToAsgiInstance:
                 value = environ[corrected_name] + "," + value
             environ[corrected_name] = value
         return environ
-
     def start_response(self, status, response_headers, exc_info=None):
         """
         WSGI start_response callable.
